first_half_evidence.possession_pressure: Both teams had 8+ turnovers by halftime; OKC had 7 fast-break points and 6 second-chance points.
first_half_evidence.model_interpretation: The halftime read was not a clean pace collapse. OKC had enough shot volume, transition leakage, offensive rebounding, and primary-creator usage to support a second-half team-total over path.

third_quarter_evidence.pace_and_foul_context: Q3 produced 70 combined points and 19 combined free throw attempts, activating pace re-acceleration and late-game foul-path logic.
third_quarter_evidence.model_interpretation: The Q3 explosion confirmed that the halftime total environment was live. Even though OKC trailed by 4 after 3Q, the competitive script created a Q4 scoring extension path for OKC.

play_by_play_evidence: OKC opened Q3 with SGA and Ajay Mitchell shot creation, including pullups, transition scoring, and foul pressure.
Lakers' Q3 run forced OKC into continued starter usage rather than bench-suppressed possessions.
Repeated lead changes/ties by end of 3Q supported a competitive fourth-quarter environment rather than a dead under script.
Tony Brothers crew context plus Q3 foul volume supported a non-zero late free-throw tail.

model_rules_applied.application: Lakers turnover volatility and OKC pressure produced enough pace/transition leakage to keep the OKC scoring path open.
Avoided a Lakers under focus and prioritized OKC scoring because Lakers scoring volatility could force OKC response possessions.
No under fired; selected OKC over instead due positive projection cross over posted line.
OKC scored 31 in Q3 and the game became competitive, strengthening the probability of fourth-quarter starter usage and late-game scoring extension.

postmortem.core_reason_hit: The model correctly identified that OKC's halftime total was not dead despite only 49 first-half points. Turnover pressure, shot-volume stability, SGA usage, Ajay Mitchell secondary creation, and third-quarter pace reacceleration created enough scoring runway to clear 107.5.
what_worked: Gamebook text allowed fast extraction of halftime efficiency, turnovers, quarter splits, foul context, and substitution/usage signals.
The model avoided the prior leak of forcing team-total unders in volatile playoff environments.
The ghost-bet discipline protected bankroll while still allowing process tracking.
what_to_refine: Exact final score should be added later for clean projection delta calculation.
When Q3 creates a plus-money re-entry on the same team total, mark whether model should add, hold, or reject based on remaining points required and price.
postmortem.forward_patch_rule: If halftime team-total over requires 58-60 second-half points but the gamebook shows active creator usage, live-ball turnover volatility, non-dead pace, and no blowout bench risk, permit ghost/min-unit over consideration when price is near even or better. If Q3 adds 30+ points and game remains within 8, upgrade to protected/validated state.
